definition: if you are behind with work, you are late, slow, or not doing as well as you should with it
example: I’m really behind with work

definition: if you cheer up, you start to feel happier. If you cheer someone up, you do something to make them feel happier
example: I’m sure she’ll cheer up soon

definition: if you are disappointed, you are slightly unhappy because something is not as good as you wanted or expected or because something did not happen the way you wanted it to
example: she sounded very disappointed when I spoke to her
I’m sorry to disappoint you, but there weren’t any tickets left
the film started well, but the ending was disappointing
she managed to hide her disappointment when she heard the result

definition: if you feel down, you feel rather sad, often for no particular reason
example: she’s been a bit down recently

definition: if something is due, it should happen or arrive at a certain time
example: the baby is due at the end of August (he or she will be born then)

definition: if you are exhausted, you are very tired
example: I was exhausted by the time we got home
he collapsed out of physical exhaustion
I just want to sleep. It’s been an exhausting day

definition: if you are fed up with something, you do not like it because it has become boring or annoying
example: I’m fed up with all this rain

definition: flu, or the flu, is an illness like a very bad cold which can become very serious. It is a short form of the medical term influenza
example: I feel awful. I think I’ve got the flu

definition: if you are furious, you are very angry
example: she was absolutely furious when she saw the mess
he protested furiously, though he knew it was hopeless
in his fury he nearly broke the door handle

definition: a ghost is the spirit of a dead person which some people say can be seen. Ghosts are considered to be very frightening, and if you say that someone looks as if they’ve seen a ghost, you mean that they look very frightened
example: a ghost story (a story that is meant to frighten the reader because it has ghosts in it)

definition: if you feel guilty about something, you feel bad because you think you have done something wrong or unhelpful. In a court, if a judge or jury says someone is guilty, they are saying officially that person committed a crime
example: I feel really guilty about leaving you to finish the work
her feelings of guilt kept her awake that night

definition: if you join someone in a social activity, you take part in it with them
example: I’m going to have a drink. Will you join me?

definition: a mark is the score or grade you get for a piece of schoolwork or an exam. When teachers mark work, they correct it and give it a score or grade
example: she expected to get a better mark after all that work
the teacher hasn’t finished marking all the exam papers yet

definition: if you are in a particular mood, that is the way you are feeling. For example, if you are in a good mood, you are happy and feel good; if you are in a bad mood, you feel bad and become angry very easily
example: she looks like she’s in a good mood today
collocations: be in no mood to do something

definition: a nightmare is a dream that is very frightening. You can refer to a really bad or worrying situation as a nightmare
example: that sounds like a nightmare

definition: pain is the physical feeling you have when a part of your body hurts because it has been hit or cut. If you say that a situation is a pain, you mean that is very annoying
example: it was such a pain – we missed the first half of the concert
translation: боль; (разг.) морока, головная боль (о чем-то раздражающем)

definition: if something is pleasant, you like it or enjoy it. If something happens pleasantly, it gives you pleasure
example: I was pleasantly surprised by the film (I liked it, although I didn’t expect to like it)
what a pleasant surprise, seeing you here!

definition: if you are pleased with something, you are happy or satisfied with it
example: you must be pleased with your exam results
the report both pleased and surprised him
what one customer might find pleasing another might find unpleasant

definition: relief is a feeling you get when a bad or worrying situation has ended and you can stop worrying
example: “I found my wallet.” – “Oh, that’s a relief!”
I felt so relieved to get home

definition: if you say that something is a shame, you mean that it is unfortunate or disappointing
example: can’t you come to the party? Oh, what a shame

definition: if two people split up, they get divorced or end their relationship. When the members of a group split up, they stop working together
example: He’s just split up with his girlfriend
after the split, we didn’t see each other for ten years

definition: if you are upset, something or someone has made you feel very sad or angry
example: he was very upset when his dog died
she didn’t know what had upset her mother
his remarks were very upsetting

definition: if something or someone bores you, they are not at all interesting to you and make you feel tired or impatient
example: his stories of the theatre never failed to bore me
she’s so boring. She only ever talks about her children
if you’re bored, let’s go out
the film was two hours of boredom

definition: your cheeks are the areas on your face under your eyes
example: she kissed me on the cheek

definition: to confuse someone means to cause them to have difficulty understanding something
example: it’ll just confuse them if you try and put too much information in at once
can you explain it again? I’m still a bit confused
the instructions for the camera were really confusing
the whole point of grammar is to avoid confusion!

definition: if something depresses you, it makes you feel sad and unhappy
example: it always depresses me when the leaves start falling off the trees
he’s been depressed ever since his wife died
all this rain is so depressing

definition: someone who is homeless has nowhere to live. People who are homeless are referred to as the homeless
example: it was really depressing to see so many homeless people on the streets
she works for a charity that helps the homeless
the policy has been successful in reducing homelessness in the capital

definition: if you hug someone, you put your arms round them and hold them tightly because you want to show them love or friendship
example: she’s always hugging me. I wish she wouldn’t
give me a hug before you go

definition: if something belongs to you, it is yours and you own it
example: who does this coat belong to?
the flood destroyed most of their belongings

definition: if something or someone drives you mad, they make you extremely annoyed
example: can you turn the music off? It’s driving me mad
translation: сводить с ума, раздражать до предела

definition: if something fails to happen, it does not happen. If you fail to do something, you do not do it
example: he failed to recognise her at first
he was criticised for his failure to communicate with his colleagues

definition: if you say “fancy!” or “fancy that!” you are expressing surprise or shock
example: fancy seeing you here!
translation: надо же!, вот это да! (выражает удивление)

definition: in some religions, hell is the place where people go after they die if they have lived a bad life. You can refer to an extremely unpleasant situation as hell
example: what’s your idea of hell?

definition: if someone is out of work, they do not have a job, although they want to have one
example: my brother is out of work at the moment

definition: if you pour a liquid, you make it flow out of its container. If it is pouring with rain, it is raining very heavily. You can also say that it’s pouring down or that the rain is pouring
example: it’s going to pour down tomorrow
translation: лить(ся); лить как из ведра (о дожде)

definition: if you set something up, you make the necessary preparations for it to start
example: he wants to set up his own business

definition: if you train for a sport, you do exercises and practice in order to become fitter and better
example: how often do you have to train?
a training session

definition: an unexpected event is surprising because no one thought it was going to happen
example: the results were totally unexpected
he died unexpectedly in his sleep
we expect to sign the contract next week

definition: your weight is how heavy you are
example: Robin has put on a lot of weight since they last met
I’m not sure how much I weigh at the moment

definition: a blanket is a large, thick piece of cloth that you use as a cover to keep yourself warm when you are in bed
example: the blanket slid off the bed during the night and I woke up really cold

definition: an issue is a subject or problem that people are discussing and trying to deal with
example: this issue needs further discussion

definition: a sheet is a large piece of cloth that you put on a bed to lie on and to cover you when you are sleeping
example: I’ll put some clean sheets on the bed
